creator: antiduh@gmail.com
text:
In reference to PR http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=173407

I got to the bottom of the weird vim and -lintl linking problem. I dug
into the autoconf crap and found out that they're being inconsistent with
use of linking flags, and this is causing autoconf to think it doesn't
need -lintl when it actually does.


I checked the config.log, and I see that my build came to the same
conclusion as Josh's: "checking for NLS... gettext() works" is what it
reports, instead of "checking for NLS... gettext() works with -lintl".

Here's what it's doing for that particular check:

configure:12291: checking for NLS
configure:12307: cc -o conftest -O2 -pipe -fno-strict-aliasing  
-I/usr/local/include  -L/usr/local/lib conftest.c -lm -lelf  -ltermlib
-liconv >&5
configure:12307: $? = 0
configure:12308: result: gettext() works

Notice the lack of -lintl on the command line. That does actually compile,
somehow, because gcc automatically links libintl. The conftest.c looks
like this:

angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) # cat conftest.c
#include <libintl.h>
int
main ()
{
    gettext("Test");
      ;
        return 0;
}


Building it with those flags works:

angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) # cc -o conftest -O2 -pipe
-fno-strict-aliasing -I/usr/local/include -L/usr/local/lib conftest.c -lm
-lelf -ltermlib -liconv
angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) #
angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) # ./conftest
angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) # echo $?
0
angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) # ldd ./conftest
./conftest:
        libm.so.5 => /lib/libm.so.5 (0x80081a000)
        libelf.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libelf.so.0 (0x800a3b000)
        libncurses.so.8 => /lib/libncurses.so.8 (0x800c51000)
        libiconv.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libiconv.so.3 (0x800e9e000)
        libc.so.7 => /lib/libc.so.7 (0x80119a000)
        libintl.so.9 => /usr/local/lib/libintl.so.9 (0x8014e0000)
angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) #



But looking at the linker command used by the end of the port:

angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/work/vim73/src) # make vim
cc -c -I. -Iproto -DHAVE_CONFIG_H   -I/usr/local/include  -O2 -pipe
-fno-strict-aliasing  -U_FORTIFY_SOURCE -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=1      
version.c -o objects/version.o
link.sh: $LINK_AS_NEEDED set to 'yes': invoking linker directly.
  cc   -L/usr/local/lib -Wl,--as-needed  -o vim objects/buffer.o 
objects/blowfish.o  objects/charset.o  objects/diff.o  objects/digraph.o
 objects/edit.o  objects/eval.o  objects/ex_cmds.o  objects/ex_cmds2.o 
objects/ex_docmd.o  objects/ex_eval.o  objects/ex_getln.o 
objects/fileio.o  objects/fold.o  objects/getchar.o  objects/hardcopy.o 
objects/hashtab.o    objects/if_cscope.o  objects/if_xcmdsrv.o 
objects/mark.o  objects/memline.o  objects/menu.o  objects/message.o 
objects/misc1.o  objects/misc2.o  objects/move.o  objects/mbyte.o 
objects/normal.o  objects/ops.o  objects/option.o  objects/os_unix.o 
objects/pathdef.o  objects/popupmnu.o  objects/quickfix.o 
objects/regexp.o  objects/screen.o  objects/search.o  objects/sha256.o 
objects/spell.o  objects/syntax.o    objects/tag.o  objects/term.o 
objects/ui.o  objects/undo.o  objects/version.o  objects/window.o       
              objects/netbeans.o    objects/main.o  objects/memfile.o   
           -lm -lelf  -ltermlib liconv

They dont have  -lintl, but they do have -Wl,--as-needed. The autoconf
shouldve used it, because when compiling the original autoconf test with
those flags, it fails without -lintl, and wouldve figured out that it has
to include it:

angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) # cc -o conftest -O2 -pipe
-fno-strict-aliasing -I/usr/local/include -L/usr/local/lib -Wl,--as-needed
conftest.c -lm -lelf -ltermlib -liconv
/tmp//ccDAqxgH.o: In function `main':
conftest.c:(.text+0xa): undefined reference to `libintl_gettext'


Tack on -lintl, and it works:

angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) # cc -o conftest -O2 -pipe
-fno-strict-aliasing -I/usr/local/include -L/usr/local/lib -Wl,--as-needed
conftest.c -lm -lelf -ltermlib -liconv -lintl
angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) # ./conftest
angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) # echo $?
0
angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) # ldd ./conftest
./conftest:
        libintl.so.9 => /usr/local/lib/libintl.so.9 (0x80081a000)
        libc.so.7 => /lib/libc.so.7 (0x800a23000)
        libiconv.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libiconv.so.3 (0x800d69000)
angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) #


Fix the autoconf to use -Wl,--as-needed when testing for anything and it
should figure out that it needs -lintl. Or heck, take out the
-Wl,--as-needed.


Also, for what it's worth:

angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) # pkg_info | grep binutils
binutils-2.23.1     GNU binary tools

angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) # cc --version
cc (GCC) 4.2.1 20070831 patched [FreeBSD]
Copyright (C) 2007 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
This is free software; see the source for copying conditions.  There is NO
warranty; not even for MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

angst(/usr/ports/editors/vim/test) #
